title: A Survey with New Results on the Spectral Interpretation of Zeros of L-functions
author: Alain Connes
approach: Spectral program: CCM operator, CvS Galerkin matrix, Weil explicit formula
critical_gaps: Convergence of det_reg to Xi function not proven (CCM 2025, Section 8)
CCM eigenvector restructures (7–22 modes) as cutoff increases — stabilization is of eigenvalue, not eigenvector
lessons: CvS Galerkin is reproducible and gives a real computational handle on the Weil form
Shannon number N_c = O(log c) bounds significant modes — explains why eigenvalue stabilizes
Eigenvector stabilization and eigenvalue stabilization are distinct; only the eigenvalue stabilizes
Suzuki Theorem 1.4 gives an unconditional RH equivalent: ||P̂Dψ||² = π⟨ψ,ψ⟩_W
our_verification: CvS Galerkin reproduced at 6 cutoffs c=13..47; eigenvalue plateau confirmed; eigenvector mode analysis done

title: Jensen polynomials for the Riemann zeta function and other sequences
author: Griffin, Ono, Rolen, Zagier
approach: Proves Jensen polynomial hyperbolicity for each fixed degree d (finitely many exceptions in n)
critical_gaps: Hyperbolicity proven for each fixed d, not for all d simultaneously — RH requires all degrees
lessons: Jensen polynomials give a concrete finite-dimensional reduction of RH at each order
The Laguerre–Pólya class is the right target; log-concavity of Taylor coefficients is related

title: The de Bruijn–Newman constant is non-negative
author: Rodgers, Tao
approach: Proves Λ ≥ 0; combined with Polymath15 Λ ≤ 0.22. RH ⟺ Λ = 0.
critical_gaps: Only shows Λ ≥ 0; proving Λ = 0 is equivalent to RH and remains open
lessons: The de Bruijn–Newman constant Λ = 0 is equivalent to RH; proving Λ ≤ 0 would suffice
The de Bruijn condition (used in our proof) is directly connected to Λ via Polya 1927

title: Even dominance of the Weil quadratic form
author: Geiger
source: Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.19035640; submitted to Communications in Mathematics 2026-03-27
approach: Even dominance of Weil QF via CAP certificates + Prime Number Theorem transfer
status: under review (2+ months, no verdict as of 2026-05)
critical_gaps: Proposition A6 interpolation is the key unverified step
Not yet peer-reviewed
lessons: CAP certificates provide a rigorous finite computational certificate — a strong model
Even dominance connects to our log-concavity approach via the Weil form
Long review period (2+ months) without rejection is mildly positive signal
our_verification: Even-dominance certificates reproduced at 6 lambda values; Proposition A6 not independently verified

title: Log-concavity approach to RH (Gershon)
author: Gershon
approach: Log-concavity of Xi kernel via Polya 1927; Lean 4 partial formalization
critical_gaps: Perturbation bound inequality goes the wrong direction (equation 16)
Claims exp(-t^4) is a counterexample to log-concavity implying real zeros — incorrect per Csordas-Varga 1989
g'' coefficient error
lessons: The perturbation bound inequality direction is a subtle but critical check — easy to get backwards
exp(-t^4) has only real zeros (Csordas-Varga 1989 Example 2.1) — do not claim it as a counterexample
Our IA must cover [0, 1.0] not just [0, 0.5]
our_verification: 3 critical issues found; perturbation bound direction wrong; counterexample claim incorrect

title: Log-concavity preprint (anonymous, April 2026)
author: Unknown
approach: Log-concavity of Xi kernel via Polya 1927; claims Lean 4 formalization
critical_gaps: Perturbation bound inequality goes wrong direction
Interval arithmetic covers [0, 0.5] only — gap at [0.5, 1.0]
g'' coefficient issue
lessons: IA coverage must span [0, 1.0] — [0, 0.5] leaves the most sensitive region uncertified
Same perturbation direction error as Gershon — this is a systematic trap in this approach
our_verification: 3 critical issues found; IA coverage only [0, 0.5]; perturbation direction wrong

title: Semilocal Spectral Descent
author: A.I. Visions LTD
approach: Claims form stabilization bypasses CCM convergence gap via eigenvector stability
critical_gaps: Eigenvector mode count changes from 7 to 22 as cutoff c increases from 13 to 47 — eigenvector is NOT stable
No peer review
lessons: Eigenvalue stabilization ≠ eigenvector stabilization — must check both
Our phase 9 (screw line) result shows H₀ space filling is consistent with eigenvector restructuring
our_verification: Eigenvector mode analysis disproves the stability assumption

title: A new AI assisted proof of the Riemann Hypothesis: A Super-Exponential Kernel Approach
author: Akram Louiz
approach: Super-exponential Möbius kernel R(c) = Sμ(c)/S(c) claimed as analytic proxy for 1/ζ(1/c); mapping s=1/c connects Re(c)>1 to critical strip
critical_gaps: R(c) → 1 identically for all c — super-exponential suppression erases all n≥2 Möbius information. R(c) does not encode ζ(s) at all
Functional equivalence R(1/s)=1/ζ(s) asserted via invalid analytic continuation from single boundary limit
Lean theorem 'analytic_louiz_kernel' uses 'sorry' — not machine-verified
Internal inconsistency: §6.1 numerics (exp(-n^c·e^c)) contradict Lean definition (exp(-n^c·exp(exp(c)))) by ~700 orders of magnitude
Spurious claim: §5.2 'disproves Twin Primes Conjecture via Brun's upper bound' — Brun's theorem does not disprove TPC
Self-validated: both papers authored by Louiz; no independent expert review
lessons: Fast convergence ≠ correct target. Super-exponential dampening is real but the ratio converges to 1, not 1/ζ(s)
Analytic continuation requires open-set agreement, not just matching at a single boundary limit
AI-generated Lean with 'sorry' is not formal proof — do not cite partial Lean scaffolds as machine verification
Internal consistency between formal definition and numerical examples is mandatory — our falsify.py exists for this
Self-validation by AI is not scientific peer review; independent falsification attempts are the standard
our_verification: Benchmark (bench_louiz_kernel.py): R(c)≈1 at all test points; §6.1 numerics reproduced with sec61 formula; Lean/sec61 inconsistency confirmed; 3 critical + 2 warning issues found

title: Spectral Determinant of a Cutoff-Regularized Hamiltonian and the Riemann Zeta Function
author: Dan Alec Yamaguchi
approach: Gram Jacobi matrix J_N: eigenvalues approximate imaginary parts of zeta zeros. Spectral determinant D_N/xi -> c via Hadamard rigidity. 77 C proof programs, 50 validation tests.
status: preprint (Zenodo, May 2026); most detailed independent attempt; not peer-reviewed
critical_gaps: Gram Jacobi eigenvectors approximate zeta zeros numerically but the analytic identification step (Theorem II: D_N/xi -> c) requires Hadamard rigidity argument not fully closed
Self-adjointness of J_infinity in the limit N->inf is asserted, not rigorously proven with domain specification
Not peer-reviewed
lessons: 77 proof-essential programs with independent verification paths (A/B/C) is a strong computational methodology
Heat kernel trace ratio 0.9999996 is strong numerical evidence but not a proof
Hadamard product convergence at 10,000 zeros is reproducible and checkable
The 'contradiction machine' (80/80 falsified) is our kind of approach — adversarial testing
our_verification: Structure reviewed; heat kernel and Hadamard claims are computationally checkable with mpmath. Analytic J_inf closure not independently verified.

title: ERURH: A Conditional, Formalized Route to the Riemann Hypothesis through ERU Framework
author: Robert Duran
approach: Energy-inertia framework on log scale s=log x. Reduces RH for xi_alpha to finite numerical certificates + classical analytic assumptions A/B/C. Full Lean 4 formalization of conditional implication ERURH_GlobalAssumptions => RH(xi_alpha).
status: preprint (HAL, Feb 2026); Lean proof is genuine but CONDITIONAL on external analytic inputs
critical_gaps: Proof is explicitly conditional: requires external proofs of analytic assumptions A (large sieve), B (spectral decay), C (RMS window hypotheses)
xi_alpha is a zeta-like function in the ERU framework, not standard Riemann zeta; connection requires Assumption 11.1
The most honest framing in this class: transparency about what is proven vs assumed is commendable
lessons: Conditional reduction to auditable finite certificates is a legitimate proof strategy
Lean can verify the conditional implication rigorously even without proving the analytic inputs
The gap between 'xi_alpha' and actual zeta function is typical of ERU-style frameworks
Explicit assumption lists make a paper more honest and auditable than implicit assumptions
our_verification: Structure reviewed. Lean conditional theorem is genuine (no sorry in the main implication per the paper). Analytic packages A/B/C not independently verified.

title: An Explicit Li-Kernel Reduction of the Riemann Hypothesis (1% Proof)
author: Sardar Dilbag Singh Khalsa
approach: Reduces RH to a single quantitative inequality: any zero with Re(s) < 1/2 produces exponentially growing contribution to Li coefficients forcing eventual negativity. Uses Laguerre-weighted explicit formula and zero-kernel representation.
status: preprint (Zenodo, Feb 2026); explicitly NOT claiming a proof of RH; honest reduction paper
critical_gaps: Explicitly does not claim proof of RH; reduces to one open inequality about global cancellations among zeros
The reduction is genuine but the inequality itself remains unproven
lessons: This is what intellectual honesty looks like: clearly stating RH is not proved but a reduction is established
The Laguerre-weighted explicit formula approach to Li coefficients is a legitimate computational tool
Papers that don't claim to prove RH but make progress are more credible than those that claim a complete proof
our_verification: Approach is honest and well-structured. Li coefficient framework is consistent with our own li_criterion.py.

title: On the Existence of the Hilbert-Polya Hamiltonian
author: Independent (arXiv 2408.15135)
source: arXiv:2408.15135 (Aug 2025, v11); most-cited of the independent arXiv attempts
approach: Constructs self-adjoint Hamiltonian R-hat = -D-hat - i*T-hat/(1+exp(-T-hat)) from Berry-Keating D-hat and Bessel operator T-hat. Eigenfunctions involve Dirichlet eta function; boundary condition Psi_s(0)=0 requires eta(s)=0, giving nontrivial Riemann zeros as eigenvalues. Self-adjointness forces eigenvalues real => RH for simple zeros.
status: arXiv preprint v11 (Aug 2025); 11 versions; not yet peer-reviewed
critical_gaps: Self-adjointness of R-hat requires careful domain analysis: D-hat and T-hat domains must be compatible, intersection may be dense but not obviously so
The argument works for SIMPLE zeros only; does not handle potential multiple zeros
Eigenfunctions for non-critical-line eta zeros (z_k = 1 + 2pik/ln2) also satisfy boundary condition, complicating the spectral analysis
lessons: Berry-Keating + Bessel operator combination is a more rigorous construction than most Hilbert-Polya attempts
Domain analysis is the critical missing step in most self-adjoint operator approaches
Multiple versions (v11) indicate ongoing refinement, which is a positive signal
Restricting claims to simple zeros is honest about the limitation
our_verification: Mathematical structure reviewed. The eta function boundary condition is correct. Domain analysis gap identified.

title: The Existence of Hilbert-Polya Operator
author: Amal Ladjeroud
source: HAL hal-05342267 (Nov 2025); Centre Universitaire de Mila, Algeria
approach: Constructs Hilbert-Polya operator H using supersymmetric quantum mechanics (SUSYQM) with shape invariance potentials. Eigenvalues are forced to be the imaginary parts of RH zeros via 'algorithmic parameters' B that update according to differences of consecutive zero imaginary parts. Proves essential self-adjointness.
critical_gaps: 'Algorithmic parameter' B is defined to produce the zeta zeros by construction — this is circular reasoning
The operator H is built to have the zeta zeros as eigenvalues by definition, not by deriving them from first principles
SUSYQM shape invariance requires the superpotential to be known; choosing it to produce zeta zeros assumes what we want to prove
lessons: Circular construction (defining an operator to have zeta zeros as eigenvalues) does not prove those zeros are on the critical line
Self-adjointness of an operator constructed this way does not imply RH unless the construction itself is RH-independent
This is a common trap: building in the conclusion as a definition
our_verification: Construction is circular: zeta zeros are inputs to the operator definition, not outputs.

title: Completion-Locked Hilbert-Polya Proof of the Riemann Hypothesis: Euler-First Rigidity
author: S.H. Meghani
source: Zenodo DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19487972 (Jan-Apr 2026, v3); claims UPenn affiliation
approach: No-tuning Hilbert-Polya approach: Archimedean completion as rigid boundary condition, zeta-regularized Fredholm determinant of positive self-adjoint operator, Euler-first rigidity on Re(s)>1, single special-value calibration. Produces companion preprints (5 related Zenodo records).
critical_gaps: 'No-tuning' claim is not rigorously established; single special-value calibration is itself a tuning
Identification of Fredholm determinant with completed zeta function requires analytic arguments not fully presented
5 companion preprints suggesting a large-scale programme not yet consolidated
lessons: 'Completion' and 'rigidity' arguments in L-function theory require careful analytic control
The volume of companion papers does not substitute for a single complete rigorous proof
our_verification: Structure reviewed. Key identification step not independently verifiable without full paper.

title: Complete Proof of RH via Dirac Operators and 600-Cell Holonomy
author: Lluis Morato de Dalmases (CronNet Holo Initiative)
approach: Dirac operator D on Hilbert space constructed from 600-cell {3,3,5} geometry. Claims: (1) RH via zeta-regularized determinant det(1/2-iD)=K*xi(1/2+iD). (2) Goldbach via heat kernel positivity. (3) Twin Primes via GUE statistics. (4) Collatz via octagonal fractal charge. Also claims Bose-Einstein condensation interpretation.
critical_gaps: Claiming RH + GRH + Goldbach + Twin Primes + Collatz simultaneously is a major red flag
Each of these is a separate multi-decade open problem; solving all five via one geometric framework is extremely implausible
600-cell angular defect delta_0 ~ 6.8 degrees used as a free parameter; connection to number theory is asserted not derived
GUE statistics of zeta zeros prove infinitude of twin primes via Keating-Smith is circular (GUE stats are consistent with RH, not a proof of it)
lessons: Claiming multiple millennium problems solved simultaneously is always a red flag
Geometric frameworks with free parameters (600-cell angular defect) that are tuned to produce the right numbers are not proofs
Physical analogies (Bose-Einstein condensation) are useful intuition but not mathematical proofs
our_verification: Not independently verified. Structural red flags (5 open problems) make deep verification low-priority.

title: On the Riemann Hypothesis via Robin Criterion
author: Cheong Siong Chua
source: Zenodo DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18323203 (Jan 2026); submitted to Duke Mathematical Journal
approach: Uses Robin's criterion: RH equivalent to sigma(n) < e^gamma * n * log(log(n)) for all n >= 5041. Attempts to prove this inequality analytically.
status: submitted to Duke Mathematical Journal (Jan 2026); no published verdict
critical_gaps: Robin's criterion is a known RH equivalent, but proving the inequality for all n requires controlling sigma(n) for highly composite numbers
Submitted to a top journal (Duke Math J) but no acceptance or referee reports shared
Using Zenodo as a timestamp before journal verdict is a common pattern; verdict unknown
lessons: Robin's criterion is a valid RH equivalent but historically very hard to leverage for a proof
Journal submission without acceptance is not validation
our_verification: Approach is legitimate (Robin's criterion is correct). Specific proof steps not independently verified.

title: The Riemann Hypothesis as a Zero-Flux Condition for a Self-Adjoint Friedrichs Operator
author: Eliahi Priest (The Priest Group Pty Ltd)
source: Zenodo DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.17228441 (Sep 2025 v2); author self-describes as 'jeweller by trade'
approach: Resonance kernel R_alpha(x)=(x-1/2)^2 * exp(-alpha*(x-1/2)^2). L^2-stable contradiction mechanism: if zero off critical line, associated flux forces L^2 divergence. Claims Clay-grade rigor.
status: preprint (Zenodo Sep 2025); not peer-reviewed; author is independent non-mathematician
critical_gaps: The resonance kernel R_alpha is centered at 1/2 by construction — this encodes the critical line assumption rather than deriving it
'L^2-stable contradiction mechanism' is asserted at high level; rigorous analytic estimates missing
Author explicitly states not a professional mathematician; proof was developed from 'pattern recognition'
lessons: Kernels or operators centered at 1/2 by construction cannot prove RH without independent justification for that centering
'Clay-grade rigor' claimed in a preprint requires actual peer review, not self-assessment
our_verification: Structural flaw identified: critical line is built into the kernel definition.

title: The phi-Separation Proof of the Riemann Hypothesis via Total Positivity
author: Timothy McGirl (AI collaborators: Claude/Grok/Gemini/GPT)
approach: phi-kernel K_phi(x)=phi^{-|x|/delta} (golden ratio base) is PF_inf (Schoenberg 1951). De Bruijn-Newman backward flow from t=1/2 (all real zeros, De Bruijn 1950) to t=0 via phi-Gram determinant monotonicity. Repulsive zero dynamics prevents collisions. Also connects to E8 lattice.
status: preprint (Zenodo Jan 2026, revised Mar 2026); AI co-authored; not peer-reviewed
critical_gaps: De Bruijn backward flow argument: starting from t=1/2 (De Bruijn 1950 says zeros are all real for t>=1/2) and going backward to t=0 requires controlling zero dynamics, not just repulsion near collisions
phi-Gram determinant monotonicity dD_N/dt > 0 requires proof that this holds for all N simultaneously
E8 lattice connection is not derived mathematically, described as providing 'scale parameter'
Four AI systems are listed as co-authors; no independent mathematical verification
lessons: De Bruijn-Newman backward flow is a legitimate approach but the non-collision argument requires more than repulsion near individual collisions
Total positivity of the phi-kernel (Schoenberg 1951) is a real result but connecting it to zeta zeros requires the analytic steps
AI co-authorship without expert mathematical review is not validation
our_verification: phi-kernel total positivity claim is correct per Schoenberg 1951. Backward flow non-collision gap identified.

title: Log-concavity of the Riemann-Jacobi Kernel Phi(u) and the Riemann Hypothesis
author: Tristen Kyle Pierson / BitConcepts Research
approach: Log-concavity of Xi kernel Phi(u) via Polya 1927 (Satz II); 52,898 IA subintervals; C=204 perturbation bound; 32 falsification attacks
critical_gaps: Lean4 formalization is a partial scaffold (not complete machine proof)
Not yet submitted for peer review
lessons: Our falsification protocol (32 attacks + external audit) is the core differentiator
Internal consistency is enforced by running falsify.py against our own proof
IA coverage [0, 1.0] with 52,898 subintervals distinguishes us from partial-coverage preprints
our_verification: Self: g'' coefficient verified (81/2), C=204 perturbation bound correct direction, IA [0,1.0] complete, cross-validation passed
